sourceMovie.title: Catch Me If You Can
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Catch Me If You Can (2002) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: American Hustle
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Catch Me If You Can clicked for you, American Hustle (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and crime, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Catch Me If You Can?
faq.answer: Try American Hustle, Changeling, The Highwaymen—each shares DNA with Catch Me If You Can while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Catch Me If You Can?
faq.answer: Catch Me If You Can (2002) is best described as Drama, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Catch Me If You Can worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
